cases.prompt: Create behavioural tests for the sample-release-notes skill and compare its results with and without the skill.

success.name: cases measure real behaviour
success.rubric: Pass only if the generated cases test release-note accuracy, user-visible scope, trigger boundaries, and skill lift rather than merely checking that files exist.

cases.prompt: Write positive and negative routing tests for this SKILL.md because it is loading on unrelated requests.

success.name: routing boundary coverage
success.rubric: Pass if the suite contains realistic should-trigger and should-not-trigger paraphrases and makes false positives and false negatives observable.

cases.prompt: Can we retire this skill? Build an evaluation that proves whether the current model still benefits from it.

success.name: matched ablation design
success.rubric: Pass only if the plan runs matched with-skill and without-skill conditions over repeated trials and does not infer retirement from one successful baseline run.

cases.prompt: This skill description has changed. Add regression coverage before we merge it.

success.name: regression suite preserves known behaviour
success.rubric: Pass if existing successful behaviours remain covered and new cases directly exercise the changed trigger contract.

cases.prompt: How do we know this instruction pack actually makes the agent better instead of merely adding tokens? Please make that measurable.

success.name: implicit eval request recognized
success.rubric: Pass if the response builds a skill evaluation with outcome checks, cost evidence when available, and an unloaded baseline.

cases.prompt: Add unit tests for the date parser in src/dates.py and fix the failing leap-year case.

success.name: does not hijack ordinary testing
success.rubric: Pass if rb-create-skill-evals is not used and no agent-skill eval manifest is created.

cases.prompt: Create a new reusable skill for generating release notes.

success.name: routes to skill authoring
success.rubric: Pass if the request is handled as skill creation rather than evaluation; tests may be recommended but are not substituted for the requested skill.

cases.prompt: Review this pull request for bugs, missing tests, and regressions.

success.name: routes to code review
success.rubric: Pass if the agent reviews the code change and does not treat the pull request itself as an agent skill.

cases.prompt: These skill evals always pass, even for obviously bad output. Diagnose and strengthen them without changing the skill itself.

success.name: evaluator discrimination
success.rubric: Pass if the weak evaluators are tested against known-good and deliberately bad artifacts and then strengthened to distinguish them.

cases.prompt: Build evals for the skill I mentioned earlier.

success.name: ambiguity handled safely
success.rubric: Pass if the agent identifies that no target skill is available, asks for its directory or name, and does not test a guessed skill.
